label: Introduction
text: Hi there. This video shows you how Balance Testing works — from creating tests all the way to showing exercises on the participant site.

label: WordPress admin
text: First, log into WordPress admin. Everything you manage is under Tests and Exercises in the left menu.

label: Tests menu
text: Under Tests you will find All Tests, Users Progress, Bulk copy, and Settings. This screen shows your test library.

label: Create a test
text: To create a balance test, go to Tests, then Add New.

label: Test content
text: Add a title, written instructions, and the test video. Choose the correct difficulty scale for that test.

label: Publish test
text: When it looks good, click Publish. Every live test still needs a matching exercise — that is the next step.

label: Link test to exercise
text: Open a published test. On the right, in the Publish box, find the button Copy To excercise — right below Update.

label: Copy To excercise
text: Click it and confirm. WordPress creates a new exercise draft with the same content copied from the test.

label: Publish exercise
text: Review the exercise, adjust the text if needed, then click Publish. That link is stored automatically.

label: Exercises list
text: On Exercises, All Exercises, you can verify the link. Each row shows an Identifier and which test it came from.

label: Tests list
text: On Tests, All Tests, check the Linked test column. It should show an exercise number — not a dash.

label: Bulk copy
text: Setting up many tests at once? Go to Tests, Bulk copy to exercises.

label: Run bulk copy
text: The page shows how many tests still need exercises. Click Run bulk copy, then publish any new exercise drafts.

label: Users Progress
text: Participants do not sign up alone — you create their accounts. Day to day, open Tests, Users Progress.

label: Open user detail
text: Each row is one person. Click VIEW to see their ratings, exercise list, and display buttons.

label: Participant login
text: On the public website, participants log in and see their account menu — not the WordPress admin.

label: Account tabs
text: Tasapainotestit is for balance tests. Harjoitukset is for exercises you have displayed. Seuraa edistymistä is for progress forms.

label: Taking a test
text: On Tasapainotestit they watch the video, read the steps, and pick a rating from one to six.

label: Ratings 3 and 4
text: Ratings three and four mean the test felt moderately hard. Those are the ratings that pick exercises for the participant.

label: Round ends
text: When the round ends, testing pauses until the next round email. You then review their suggested exercises.

label: Rating recap
text: In Users Progress you can see every rating. Three and four suggest exercises. One and two do not come back. Six removes a test forever.

label: Exercise suggestions
text: After a round, up to five exercises appear as Suggested — one per test they rated three or four, if that test has a linked exercise.

label: Approve
text: Open Exercise assignments. Click Approve on each row you want, or use Approve all suggested.

label: Reorder
text: Use the arrow buttons or drag the handle to change order. The top row shows first on the participant site.

label: Add manually
text: You can search and add an exercise manually if needed. Manual adds are approved right away.

label: Display exercises
text: Approving is not enough. Click the blue Display exercises button — that is what sends exercises to the participant.

label: Participant email
text: They get an email that exercises are ready, and the Harjoitukset tab opens on their account.

label: Settings
text: Under Tests, Settings you set the exercise countdown days and delays between rounds — default is twelve days.

label: Harjoitukset tab
text: This is what the participant sees — your approved exercises in order, plus how many days are left to practice.

label: Waiting message?
text: If they only see a waiting message, you likely approved exercises but forgot to click Display exercises.

label: Summary
text: That is the full loop — create tests, link exercises, participants rate tests, you approve, reorder, and display.

label: Documentation
text: Full written guide at mdhemalakanda dot github dot io slash balance-testing. Thanks for watching.
